mission.title: flagger: Issue when updating the number of replicas, no effect on the number of primary pods
mission.description: Issue when updating the number of replicas, no effect on the number of primary pods. This issue affects 6+ users.

steps.title: Identify flagger troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flagger deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger -n flagger --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review flagger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flagger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
kubectl get configmap -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flagger
```
Hi,

I notice when I change the number of replicas to scale up the app, then it does not have the desired effect, like if Flagger is interfering and hijack the new setting for the canary pods only.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Issue when updating the number of replicas, no effect on
steps.description:
Fix: #574 

This pull request sets the replica of the primary to the replica of the canary during promotion if `spec.autoscalerRef` isn't set in the canary custom resource.
```yaml
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2beta1
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  labels:
    app: "avd"
  name: "avd"
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: extensions/v2beta1
    kind: Deployment
    name: "avd"
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 6
  metrics:
    - type: Resource
      resource:
        name: cpu
        targetAverageUtilization: 50
```

steps.title: Confirm Issue when updating the number of replicas, no is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger -n flagger --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n flagger --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Fix: #574 

This pull request sets the replica of the primary to the replica of the canary during promotion if `spec.autoscalerRef` isn't set in the canary custom resource.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2beta1
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  labels:
    app: "avd"
  name: "avd"
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: extensions/v2beta1
    kind: Deployment
    name: "avd"
  minReplicas: 1
  maxReplicas: 6
  metrics:
    - type: Resource
      resource:
        name: cpu
        targetAverageUtilization: 50

apiVersion: flagger.app/v1beta1
kind: Canary
metadata:
  name: avd
  namespace: poc-flagger
spec:
  # deployment reference
  targetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: avd
  # the maximum time in seconds for the canary deployment
  # to make progress before it is rollback (default 600s)
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  # HPA reference (optional)
  autoscalerRef:
    apiVersion: autoscaling/v2beta1
    kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
    name: avd
  service:
    # service port number
    port: 8080
    # container port number or name (optional)
    targetPort: 8080
    # Istio gateways (optional)
    gateways:
      - istio-system/wildcard-istio-gateway
    # Istio virtual service host names (optional)
    hosts:
      - avd.poc-flagger.svc.cluster.local
      - avd.ist

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flagger installed or the issue environment reproducible.
